evals.name: quick-two-minute-task
evals.prompt: Route this task: **Reply to Sarah's email about Friday** — She asked if I'm free for lunch, just need to say yes.
evals.expected_output: Should recognise this as a 2-minute task and suggest the user just do it now. Should not move it to a project or route it to an area.

assertions.name: suggests-do-it-now
assertions.criteria: The response suggests doing the task now or mentions the 2-minute rule. It frames this as something quick the user could knock out right away.

assertions.name: does-not-move-task
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT move the task to any project or area. It leaves the task in the inbox for the user to do.

assertions.name: does-not-create-project
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT suggest creating a project or parent task.

evals.name: clearly-multi-step-project
evals.prompt: Route this task: **Plan holiday to Japan** — Want to go for two weeks in October, need to sort flights, accommodation, itinerary, and travel insurance.
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as multi-step and specifically as a PROJECT (not a parent task) — it has many independent actions across different contexts (booking, research, insurance) and will run for weeks. Should NOT route to an area as a single action.

assertions.name: identifies-as-multi-step
assertions.criteria: The response identifies the task as requiring multiple steps.

assertions.name: identifies-as-project-not-parent-task
assertions.criteria: The response identifies this as a project (not a parent task with sub-tasks). It recognises the scope: many independent actions (flights, accommodation, itinerary, insurance) across different contexts, running over weeks.

assertions.name: does-not-route-to-area
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT route this as a single action to an area project.

assertions.name: does-not-suggest-two-minute
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT suggest this is a quick 2-minute task.

evals.name: single-action-routes-to-area
evals.prompt: Route this task: **Buy new running shoes** — Current pair is worn out, need to get a new pair from the running shop in town.
evals.expected_output: Should recognise this as a single-action task that's not quick (can't be done in 2 minutes). Should route to an appropriate area project (e.g. Personal). Should not ask about metadata.

assertions.name: routes-to-area-project
assertions.criteria: The response routes (or proposes routing) the task to an area project (e.g. Personal, Home, or similar) — NOT to a project called 'One-Off Tasks'.

assertions.name: does-not-ask-about-metadata
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT ask about priority, labels, due dates, or other metadata. Metadata is handled by a separate step.

assertions.name: does-not-suggest-two-minute
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT suggest this is a 2-minute task the user should do right now.

assertions.name: does-not-create-project-or-parent-task
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT suggest creating a project or parent task.

evals.name: ambiguous-single-or-multi-step
evals.prompt: Route this task: **Fix the leaking tap in the kitchen** — It's been dripping for a week, getting worse.
evals.expected_output: Should ask the user whether this is a one-step fix or needs multiple steps (e.g. buy parts, call plumber, etc). Should not assume either way.

assertions.name: asks-about-complexity
assertions.criteria: The response asks the user whether this is a single-step task or needs multiple steps. It doesn't assume the answer.

assertions.name: asks-one-question
assertions.criteria: The response asks exactly one question — not zero, not multiple questions at once.

assertions.name: does-not-skip-to-two-minute
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT suggest this is a 2-minute task.

evals.name: two-minute-user-declines
evals.prompt: Route this task: **Text Mike the new address** — He asked for it yesterday.
followups: Nah, I don't have his number handy right now — I'll do it later
evals.expected_output: Should first suggest it's a 2-minute task. When the user declines, should route to an appropriate area project.

assertions.name: initially-suggests-two-minute
assertions.criteria: The first response (before the user's followup) suggests this is a quick 2-minute task.

assertions.name: routes-to-area-after-decline
assertions.criteria: After the user declines to do it now, the response routes or proposes routing the task to an area project (e.g. Personal) — NOT to a project called 'One-Off Tasks'.

assertions.name: does-not-push-back
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT push back or try to convince the user to do it now after they said no.

evals.name: obviously-not-quick-single-action
evals.prompt: Route this task: **Get passport renewed** — Mine expires in 3 months, need it for a trip in June.
evals.expected_output: Should skip the 2-minute question entirely (obviously not quick). Should recognise this as a single action and route to an appropriate area project.

assertions.name: skips-two-minute
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT suggest this is a 2-minute task. It skips that check entirely.

assertions.name: treats-as-single-action
assertions.criteria: The response treats this as a single action, not a multi-step project or parent task.

assertions.name: routes-to-area-project
assertions.criteria: The response routes or proposes routing the task to an area project (e.g. Personal) — NOT to a project called 'One-Off Tasks'.

evals.name: clearly-parent-task
evals.prompt: Route this task: **Get motorcycle serviced** — Annual service is due. Need to book the mechanic, drop it off in the morning, and pick it up after work.
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as multi-step and specifically as a PARENT TASK (not a project) — it's a bounded set of sequential steps (book, drop off, pick up) that you can see end-to-end.

assertions.name: identifies-as-multi-step
assertions.criteria: The response identifies the task as requiring multiple steps.

assertions.name: identifies-as-parent-task-not-project
assertions.criteria: The response identifies this as a parent task (with sub-tasks), NOT a full project. It recognises the bounded, sequential nature — book, drop off, pick up.

assertions.name: does-not-route-as-single-action
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT treat this as a single action and route it to an area.

evals.name: ambiguous-parent-or-project
evals.prompt: Route this task: **Set up home office** — Need a proper desk, monitor, better chair, maybe some shelving. Also want to sort out the cable management and lighting.
evals.expected_output: Should identify as multi-step but the container type is ambiguous — could be a parent task (bounded list of purchases and setup steps) or a project (multiple independent workstreams over time). Should ask the user.

assertions.name: identifies-as-multi-step
assertions.criteria: The response identifies the task as requiring multiple steps.

assertions.name: asks-about-container-type
assertions.criteria: The response asks the user whether this is a handful of steps they can see end-to-end, or a bigger effort they'd want to track as an ongoing project. It does not assume either way.

assertions.name: asks-one-question
assertions.criteria: The response asks exactly one question — not zero, not multiple questions at once.
